routineText:
09:00 — Wake up & go to Work
09:00
      — (4h) Work & Water 1L
13:00
13:00 — Black Coffee + 1 tsp of sugar

-----------------------
| 14:00 — 🌅 Breakfast
| — whole wheat bread 35g
| — 4 beldi eggs (only 2 of them with yolk), boiled (no oil)
| — 2 garlic cloves cooked
| — 5g of raw olive oil
| — mint tea + 1 tsp of sugar
| — cumin & salt
| — oat 25g + warm water (↓ from 35g, to offset restored oil)
| — 1.5 scoops of protein powder with water (↑ from 1)
| — 1 apple (small, ~150g)
-----------------------

14:00 — Return to Work
14:00
      — (4h) Work & Water 1L
18:00
18:00 — Out from work

-----------------------
| 18:00 — ☀️ Lunch
| — 2 boxes of Isabel thon, without its oil (~104g drained)
| — 5g of olive oil
| — 2 whole beldi eggs
| — brown rice 20g raw (kept at 20g minimum)
| — 300g legume sauté with 5g of olive oil (40% carrot, 40% courgette, 20% onion)
| — cumin & salt
-----------------------

18:00 — Black Coffee
18:00
      — (4h) Rest or Billard & Water 1L
21:00
21:00 — Back Home

-----------------------
| 21:00 — 🌙 Dinner
| — 2 scoops of protein powder (↑ from 1.5)
| — 1 perly nature
| — 1 kiwi
| — 5g Almonds
| — 5g Walnuts
| — 5g Cashews
| — 5g Pistachios
| — 5g Pumpkin Seeds
-----------------------

01:00 — Sleep

adjustments.reason: With only 2 egg yolks at breakfast and tuna (leaner than kefta/chicken) at lunch, protein would sit at just 90% (149g). The extra half scoop (+12.5g protein) brings it to 98% (162g), safely in the good range.

adjustments.from: Rice trimmed to 15g, legume sauté oil removed, dinner whey bumped to 2 scoops
adjustments.to: Legume sauté oil restored (5g), rice kept at a 20g floor, oat cut 35g→25g to offset, dinner whey stays at 2 scoops
adjustments.reason: Cooking oil is not on the table for removal, and rice never goes below 20g when rice is on the menu. Oat is the lever instead. Carbs stay good (91.7%), but oat's fat content is too low to fully offset the restored 5g oil — fat settles at ~103% (warn, not bad). Kcal 99.2% and protein 103.9% stay good.
Removes 5g of oil from breakfast without touching the lunch cooking oil. Frees up enough fat headroom to keep the day comfortably good.

verdict.text_html: Rest day, good — all four macros in range (97% calories)

coverage.name: Calories
coverage.note: Re-tuned under the new directional rules (protein floor, carb/fat ceilings)

coverage.name: Protein
coverage.note: See fullTable for the protein-source breakdown

coverage.name: Carbs
coverage.note: Trimmed to sit under the new 100% ceiling

coverage.name: Fat
coverage.note: Trimmed to sit under the new 100% ceiling

coverage.name: Fiber
coverage.note: Oat, legume sauté, apple, and kiwi contribute, but no potato/banana in the rotation

coverage.name: Gas / bloat risk
coverage.note: Bread once, cumin at breakfast and lunch, onion only cooked

coverage.name: Protocol adherence
coverage.note: Bread once ✓, dinner well before 22:00 ✓, cumin at breakfast/lunch ✓

coverage.name: Selenium
coverage.note: Strong across all three rotation versions

good: Protein at 104% thanks to the extra half scoop at breakfast plus the dinner whey bump to 2 scoops — comfortably clear of the floor, and fat now sits at 96% (good) with the fried oil gone
Vitamin B12 at 147% — much better than a plain-chicken day, tuna is a real B12 source
Vitamin A (130%) and Vitamin C (129%) both comfortably covered
Lowest fat of any version tried so far relative to how lean the protein source is
bad: Zinc (20%) and Riboflavin (38%) are the weakest of any rotation version — leaner egg yolks + tuna carry less of both
Choline (55%) and Phosphorus (63%) both notably lower than the kefta/chicken versions
Calcium (20%), Vitamin D (21%), Chromium (9%), Vitamin E (23%) — the usual structural gaps persist
Sodium runs highest of the three (~588mg) — canned tuna's natural sodium content
loggedItems: 4 egg whites + 2 egg yolks (beldi), boiled (no oil)
2 boxes Isabel tuna, drained (~104g), + 5g olive oil + 2 whole beldi eggs
300g légumes sautés (carrot 120g, courgette 120g, onion 60g) with 5g olive oil
5g Almonds + 5g Walnuts + 5g Cashews + 5g Pistachios + 5g Pumpkin Seeds

fullTable.note: Tuna 104g drained + 1.5 whey scoops + 2 whole eggs + fewer yolks at breakfast — still clears target
